name: Voltage High Society
short_description: A first-person Metroidvania set on a prison island crawling with cybernetic horrors. Punch your way through interconnected zones, evolve deadly new abilities, and fight to escape Nemo Ultra. Inspired by Tetsuo: The Iron Man.
detailed_description:
EARLY ACCESS ROADMAP

ROAD TO 1.0

  • [DONE] Core combat system

  • [DONE] Full ability and weapon roster

  • [DONE] Major boss encounters (all but the final boss)

  • [IN PROGRESS] Final story content and ending

  • [IN PROGRESS] New collectible system

  • [IN PROGRESS] Final polish pass

  • [PLANNED] Launch day patch with remaining secrets


About the Game

NEMO ULTRA. YOUR PRISON. YOUR PROVING GROUND.

You wake up on a decaying island overrun by cybernetic monsters. No guns. No allies. Just your fists and whatever you can rip from the things trying to kill you.

Voltage High Society is a first-person Metroidvania. Explore 30+ interconnected areas, find weapons and abilities that unlock new paths, and fight your way to freedom through a world inspired by Tetsuo: The Iron Man and Escape from New York.

This is not a shooter with a map. This is a Metroidvania built from the ground up in first person: locked doors, ability gates, backtracking, shortcuts, secrets, and boss fights that test everything you've learned.

PUNCH FIRST. UPGRADE LATER.

Combat starts with your fists. Every new weapon and ability you find changes how you fight and where you can go. Melee-focused first-person combat rewards aggression and positioning, not camping behind cover.

5 LARGE AREAS. ONE CONNECTED WORLD.

Nemo Ultra is a dense, hand-crafted world built for exploration. Dead ends become shortcuts. Locked passages open when you find the right ability. Every zone connects, and the map rewards players who pay attention.

BODY HORROR MEETS RETRO GRIT

The visual style blends PS1-era retro aesthetics with modern lighting and creature design. Dark, oppressive corridors open into neon-lit arenas. The monsters are grotesque, the atmosphere is thick, and the world feels like a place that existed before you got there.

YOUR BODY IS YOUR PROGRESSION

New abilities are not just combat upgrades. They're keys to the world. Every power you gain reopens areas you've already explored, revealing hidden routes and secrets you missed the first time through.
